title: psilochenia modocensis subsp. subacaulis
intro: Psilochenia modocensis subsp. subacaulis, commonly known as Modoc pine, is a plant species that belongs to the Pinaceae family. It is native to North America and has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Psilochenia modocensis subsp. subacaulis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of the plant include terpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic actions such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-microbial effects.
side_effects: Psilochenia modocensis subsp. subacaulis can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches. In some cases, excessive consumption may lead to increased heart rate, anxiety, and panic attacks. It can also cause visual disturbances, including blurred vision and altered perception. Furthermore, improper use may lead to long-term psychological effects, including depression and psychosis in extreme cases.
